name: Owings Auto
description_short: Owings Auto is a Buy Here Pay Here used car dealership in Arlington and Fort Worth, TX, offering in-house financing for bad-credit buyers with low down payments.
description_long:
Owings Auto is a family-run Buy Here Pay Here (BHPH) used car dealership that has served the Fort Worth and Arlington, Texas area for more than 40 years. It operates two physical locations — one on Division Street in Arlington and one on Jacksboro Highway in Fort Worth — and specializes in selling used vehicles to consumers who cannot qualify for traditional bank or credit union auto loans. The business model centers on in-house financing, meaning Owings Auto itself acts as both the dealer and the lender.

Owings Auto sells a range of used trucks, SUVs, and sedans with financing arranged directly through its own finance department. Buyers with bad credit, no credit history, past bankruptcies, foreclosures, divorces, or prior vehicle repossessions are explicitly welcomed. The dealership offers a simple online credit application for pre-approval and an online payment portal for existing customers. Select vehicles are bundled with vehicle service contracts covering 12 months/12,000 miles or 24 months/24,000 miles, with coverage that includes the engine, transmission, water pump, fuel pump, and radiator — not just powertrain.

What sets Owings Auto apart within the BHPH segment is its longevity — over four decades in the same local market — and its stated willingness to work with virtually any credit situation. The service contract offering on select vehicles is more comprehensive than typical BHPH guarantees, and same-day approval and drive-off is available. The dealership positions itself as a local, relationship-driven alternative to large financial institutions for buyers who are locked out of conventional auto financing.

Buyers should understand that BHPH dealerships typically charge significantly higher interest rates than banks or credit unions — often 20–30% APR or higher — and prices on vehicles may be above market rate to offset lending risk. Owings Auto does not publish its interest rates, loan terms, or vehicle pricing online, which makes it impossible to compare costs before visiting a location. This model can work well for consumers who have no other path to vehicle ownership but carries a higher long-term cost. It is not a personal loan lender in the traditional sense; financing is exclusively tied to vehicle purchase. Many lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months. For those with damaged credit, credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus. Secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to rebuilding credit scores over time.
pros: In-house financing with no reliance on third-party banks — approval decisions made on-site
Over 40 years in business serving the Fort Worth and Arlington, TX market
Accepts buyers with bad credit, bankruptcy, foreclosure, repossession, or no credit history
Low down payments advertised — does not require large upfront cash
Vehicle service contracts available on select vehicles covering engine, transmission, fuel pump, radiator, and more (not just powertrain)
Two physical locations in DFW metro — Arlington and Fort Worth
Same-day approval and drive-off possible based on website claims
cons: Interest rates, APR, and loan terms are not disclosed online — impossible to compare costs before visiting
Inventory and pricing not fully detailed online — limited transparency on vehicle values
BHPH model typically carries significantly higher interest rates than conventional auto lenders (often 20–30%+ APR)
Financing is only available for vehicle purchases — not a general-purpose loan provider
No publicly listed customer reviews, third-party ratings, or BBB accreditation status on the website
best_for: Consumers with bad credit, recent bankruptcy, or repossession who cannot qualify at a bank or credit union
Buyers who need a vehicle quickly and want same-day approval and same-day purchase
DFW-area residents who prefer to work with a long-established local dealer rather than a large institution
First-time buyers with no credit history who need in-house financing to establish a payment record
diagnosis: Owings Auto is best for DFW-area consumers with damaged or no credit who need a vehicle and have been turned away by traditional lenders — it is a last-resort or credit-rebuilding option, not a competitive financing product. The main caveat is that BHPH loans carry high interest rates that are not disclosed upfront, meaning total cost of ownership can be substantially higher than a conventional auto loan; buyers should negotiate carefully and understand the full loan terms before signing.
services: Buy Here Pay Here used vehicle sales
Vehicle service contracts (12 mo/12K mi and 24 mo/24K mi options)
Two showroom locations (Arlington and Fort Worth, TX)
